macro.takeaway: The U.S. economy is still growing, but not fast enough to make the Fed comfortable cutting rates quickly.

source.name: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (GDP advance estimate)

gold.takeaway: Gold held near record-high levels even as markets priced in fewer near-term rate cuts.

china.takeaway: China data looked soft (services PMI 49.4), and A-shares are closed May 1–5 for Labour Day so this uses the US-listed CSI 300 ETF proxy.

thailand.takeaway: Thailand’s policy rate is still 1.00%, so the BoT is staying supportive even as inflation picks up.

us.takeaway: Stocks finished the week near record highs while the Fed kept rates at 3.5%–3.75%.
